zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice de Staunton於大約1300年出生在英國史洛普爾邱奇 斯特頓時，她的父親William de Staunton66歲，母親Isabel De Kirketon62歲。她於大約1320年與John ap Rhys le Cambray在威爾士結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世在英國史洛普爾邱奇 斯特頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Staunton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Caitlin, Ciaran, Cormac.

English and Irish: variant of Stanton . This English name has been established (in this spelling) since medieval times in Ireland (Mayo, Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as de Stonndún. Compare McEvilly .

variants.name: McEvilly
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Mhílidh ‘son of the knight’, from Gaelic míleadh ‘warrior, knight’. In County Mayo and County Cork, a family of English origin Gaelicized their name from Staunton to Mac an Mhílidh, apparently because of the latter's presumed similarity to the name of their ancestor, Milo de Stanton.

variants.name: Stanton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in England called Stanton or Staunton, especially Stanton in Staffordshire and Staunton in Worcestershire, all named with Old English stān ‘stone’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Most of the placenames come from their situation on stony ground, but in the case of Stanton Harcourt in Oxfordshire and Stanton Drew in Avon the reference is to the proximity of prehistoric stone monuments. The name has also sometimes been chosen by Ashkenazic Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames. This surname has also long been established in Ireland (especially Mayo).

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice de Staunton was born about 1300, in Church Stretton, Shropshire, England, her father, William de Staunton, was 66 and her mother, Isabel De Kirketon, was 62. She married John ap Rhys le Cambray about 1320, in Wales. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in Church Stretton, Shropshire, England.

Elle a épousé John ap Rhys le Cambray environ 1320, à Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée à Church Stretton, Shropshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice de Stauntonが約1300年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアチャーチ ストレットオンで生まれた時，父William de Stauntonは66歳，母Isabel De Kirketonは62歳であった。約1320年にウェールズでJohn ap Rhys le Cambrayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスシュロプシュアイアチャーチ ストレットオンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Staunton(이)가 대략 1300년 영국 슈럽실르 처르치 스트레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Staunton(은)는 66세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel De Kirketon(은)는 62세였다. 그녀는 대략 1320년 웨일스에서 John ap Rhys le Cambray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 슈럽실르 처르치 스트레턴에서 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John ap Rhys le Cambray около 1320 г. в местности Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в местности чурч стреттон, шропшир, Англия.
